Politics.headline: Minister Joly to participate in fireside chat with President of Finland
Politics.detail: The Honourable Mélanie Joly, Minister of Industry and Minister responsible for Canada Economic Development for Quebec Regions, will participate in a fireside chat with Alexander Stubb, President of Finland, to discuss Arctic capabilities, defence and security in Ottawa.

Politics.headline: Minister Gull-Masty to join the first Walking Out Ceremony on Parliament Hill
Politics.detail: The Honourable Mandy Gull-Masty, Minister of Indigenous Services, will celebrate the first Walking Out Ceremony on Parliament Hill—an important rite of passage for a Cree child—followed by a media scrum.

Politics.headline: The latest from the Presidency of the Council of Ministers
Politics.detail: The Presidency of the Council of Ministers provides updates and information on its activities.

Politics.headline: Our democracy is not 'set and forget'
Politics.detail: Australia's democracy is strong but faces challenges like misinformation, polarization, and economic shocks that require constant attention and reform.

Politics.headline: Reeves condemns Trump's decision to launch war against Iran as 'folly'
Politics.detail: Rachel Reeves has criticized Trump's decision to initiate military action against Iran, calling it a misguided and unwise action.

Finance & Economy.headline: IMF Cuts 2026 Global Growth Outlook
Finance & Economy.detail: The IMF has revised its 2026 global growth forecast downward to 3.1%, a 0.2 percentage-point cut from previous expectations, reflecting the economic fallout from the ongoing Middle East conflict. Prolonged hostilities could further weaken the global economy and destabilize financial markets.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Producer Prices Rise Less than Expected in March
Finance & Economy.detail: US producer prices increased by 0.5% month-over-month in March 2026, matching the previous period's growth but falling short of market expectations of 1.1%. Goods prices surged 1.6%, the largest increase since August 2023, fueled by an 8.5% jump in energy costs, largely attributed to the ongoing Iran conflict.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Small Business Optimism Lowest in 11 Months
Finance & Economy.detail: The NFIB Small Business Optimism Index in the US fell to 95.8 points in March 2026, the lowest since April 2025. The reading is now below its 52-year average of 98, as the dramatic spike in oil prices has spooked consumers and owners alike.

Finance & Economy.headline: Reeves arrives at IMF with little leeway to prove its UK downgrade wrong
Finance & Economy.detail: Rachel Reeves is at the IMF, but has little room to maneuver given the UK's economic downgrade. The article discusses the challenges and limited options available to Reeves in light of the economic situation.

Finance & Economy.headline: China, Iran weaponized the global economy to beat the U.S. at its own game
Finance & Economy.detail: Washington once enjoyed a near monopoly over this type of economic warfare, but now American consumers and companies are starting to feel the pain.

Technology.headline: Meta doubles down on partnership with Broadcom on custom AI processors
Technology.detail: Meta is extending its partnership with Broadcom to design in-house AI accelerators, committing to an initial deployment of 1 gigawatt's worth of its Meta Training and Inference Accelerators (MTIA). The MTIA chips will utilize Broadcom's chip design, packaging, and networking technologies to build a massive computing foundation for delivering personal superintelligence.

Technology.headline: Hong Kong tech expos spotlight cutting-edge innovation, forge partnerships
Technology.detail: Hong Kong's technology expos are highlighting cutting-edge innovations and fostering partnerships in the tech industry. These events serve as a platform for showcasing the latest advancements and facilitating collaborations within the global technology landscape.

Technology.headline: Fujitsu and Chugoku Electric Power T&D sign IP licensing agreement to accelerate renewable energy integration and advance power transmission maintenance
Technology.detail: Fujitsu and Chugoku Electric Power Transmission & Distribution Co., Inc. have signed an intellectual property licensing agreement to expand renewable energy adoption and enhance power transmission facility maintenance. Fujitsu will leverage Chugoku Electric Power T&D's technology to launch an advanced power grid operation and maintenance support service, enabling safe and maximum utilization of renewable energy.

Technology.headline: Tesla leader believes Shanghai factory operations will play a role in robot mass production
Technology.detail: A Tesla leader believes its Shanghai factory operations will help resolve the challenges in achieving mass production of the company's humanoid robots. The Shanghai factory's operations are expected to contribute significantly to the mass production of Tesla's humanoid robots.

Technology.headline: Why Anthropic's Mythos AI poses 'catastrophic' cybersecurity risk to banks running antiquated tech
Technology.detail: Anthropic's Mythos, a new AI model, is identified by the company and cybersecurity experts as a potential supercharger for complex cyberattacks, posing significant challenges to banks with legacy technology systems. Mythos's high-level coding capabilities can identify and exploit cybersecurity vulnerabilities, creating a problem for financial institutions using both state-of-the-art and decades-old software.

Health.headline: AI chatbots give misleading medical advice 50% of the time, study finds
Health.detail: A new study reveals that AI chatbots provide problematic medical advice half of the time, posing health risks as this technology becomes more integrated into daily life, with nearly 20% of responses being highly problematic. The chatbots performed better on closed-ended prompts related to vaccines and cancer but struggled with open-ended prompts and topics like stem cells and nutrition.

Health.headline: 'Aroha in action': Community gifts new home to health worker after fire
Health.detail: A Waikato community is supporting a Māori health worker who lost her home in a fire by gifting her a repurposed house, reflecting kaupapa Māori values. Te Kōhao Health is relocating the house to Billie Gillet-Kati's land in Waharoa, recognizing her dedication to the community.

Health.headline: UC graduate with severe allergy to enter medical school
Health.detail: Eddie Gerstner, a University of Cincinnati graduate with a severe milk allergy and eosinophilic esophagitis, is set to attend medical school after managing life-threatening reactions throughout his life.  Gerstner carries four EpiPens and has navigated numerous health challenges, including a week-long ICU stay due to an allergic reaction in fifth grade.

Health.headline: New trial over Diego Maradona's death resumes in Argentina against 7 health care professionals
Health.detail: A new trial has resumed in Argentina against seven health care professionals in connection to the death of Diego Maradona. The proceedings aim to determine if negligence or malpractice contributed to the football legend's passing.

Health.headline: Emergency medicine in the spotlight: The Pitt's efforts to paint an accurate picture
Health.detail: The University of Pittsburgh is working to provide a more accurate portrayal of emergency medicine, challenging the misconception that emergency departments are filled with patients with non-urgent needs. Emergency physicians are highly skilled at quickly shifting focus between patients, but the work is very demanding.

Sports.headline: Dembele hails PSG's ability to 'suffer' in win over Liverpool
Sports.detail: Ousmane Dembele praised his Paris Saint-Germain teammates for their resilience in defeating Liverpool 2-0 at Anfield in the Champions League quarter-final, securing a 4-0 aggregate victory and a spot in the semi-finals. He acknowledged the difficulty of playing in such a stadium despite their first-leg advantage.

Sports.headline: Atletico 'ready' for Champions League success at last: Simeone
Sports.detail: Atletico Madrid coach Diego Simeone expressed confidence in his team's readiness to win the Champions League after their victory over Barcelona in the quarter-finals. Despite a 2-1 defeat in the second leg, they progressed with a 3-2 aggregate score and will face Arsenal or Sporting Lisbon in the semi-finals.

Sports.headline: BASEBALL / Teng Kai-wei to shoulder more amid Houston Astros' pitching shortage
Sports.detail: Houston Astros pitcher Teng Kai-wei, the only active Taiwanese player in MLB, is expected to take on a larger role due to the team's injury-plagued pitching staff. General Manager Dana Brown mentioned that the team plans to increase the responsibility of bullpen pitchers, including Teng.

Sports.headline: KKR's Winless Run In IPL 2026 Continues, Slumps To 32-Run Loss Against CSK
Sports.detail: Kolkata Knight Riders suffered a 32-run loss against Chennai Super Kings in IPL 2026, extending their winless streak. Chennai Super Kings climbed above Mumbai Indians in the latest IPL 2026 points table with this victory.

Sports.headline: Sanju Samson named ICC men's Player of the Month for March
Sports.detail: Sanju Samson has been awarded the ICC Men's Player of the Month for March. This recognition highlights his outstanding performance in recent matches.

Entertainment.headline: Britney Spears enters rehab after March DUI arrest
Entertainment.detail: Pop star Britney Spears has voluntarily checked into rehab following her DUI arrest in Ventura County in March, a representative for the singer told CBS LA.

Entertainment.headline: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame: Phil Collins, Oasis, Sade and Wu-Tang Clan among 2026 inductees
Entertainment.detail: Phil Collins, Iron Maiden, Billy Idol, Queen Latifah, Oasis, Sade and Joy Division/New Order will be inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, along with first-time nominees Wu-Tang Clan and the late Luther Vandross.

Entertainment.headline: Tom Holland Declares Zendaya 'Looks Amazing' as They Step Out in Paris Amid Marriage Speculation
Entertainment.detail: Tom Holland tells fans Zendaya 'looks amazing' as they step out ahead of her "The Drama' premiere in Paris amid marriage speculation.

Entertainment.headline: JAY-Z Breaks Silence on Being Accused in Diddy Lawsuits
Entertainment.detail: JAY-Z breaks his silence about being named in a 2024 sexual misconduct lawsuit tied to Sean 'Diddy' Combes in a new 'GQ' interview.

Entertainment.headline: Bill Cosby Found Liable in Sexual Assault Case, Owes Victim $59M
Entertainment.detail: Bill Cosby is ordered to pay $59 million in damages to Donna Motsinger for drugging and assaulting her in 1972.

Crime & Justice.headline: Albanian crime gang jailed following UK wide burglary series
Crime & Justice.detail: Four Albanian men and a British woman have been sentenced to more than 38 years in prison for their roles in a series of high value burglaries across the UK, where they ransacked properties stealing jewellery, designer handbags, watches and cash.

Crime & Justice.headline: Prolific criminal re-offended days after court warning
Crime & Justice.detail: Robert Rust, 38, was jailed for six months after carrying out a spree of raids on businesses just days after getting a suspended sentence for similar offences, including stealing a locked bike, attempting to break into a barbershop, and stealing from a Chinese takeaway and a Co-op.

Crime & Justice.headline: Sam Altman attack suspect appears in court
Crime & Justice.detail: The 20-year-old man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home made his first court appearance on Tuesday and is now facing multiple charges, including attempted murder.

Crime & Justice.headline: 1 dead, 6 hurt in Chick-fil-A shooting, New Jersey officials say.
Crime & Justice.detail: A mass shooting at Chick-fil-A in Union Township, New Jersey, left one person dead and six others wounded.

Crime & Justice.headline: Woman with family ties to Colorado added to FBI's Ten Most Wanted list.
Crime & Justice.detail: KaShawn Roper is accused of shooting two women — killing one — in western Missouri in August 2020.

Education.headline: China aims to build an AI literacy system
Education.detail: China has launched an action plan to create a comprehensive AI literacy system across all education levels, integrating AI into the national education system and ensuring equal access to AI learning opportunities for all citizens.

Education.headline: UTM signs vocational education cooperation deal with 4 local schools
Education.detail: The Macao University of Tourism (UTM) has partnered with four local secondary schools to introduce tailored vocational education programs focused on tourism and service industries, combining theoretical learning with practical training to develop skilled professionals.

Education.headline: UAE announces readiness for gradual return to in-person learning for schools and nurseries
Education.detail: The UAE's Education, Human Resources and Community Development Council has announced preparations for a phased return to in-person learning, starting with nurseries, while further details on the learning mode for other institutions will be released on April 15, 2026.

Education.headline: Breaking the cycle: How the education department plans to keep pregnant learners in school
Education.detail: South Africa's Department of Basic Education is seeking to permanently end the expulsion of pregnant learners through new draft regulations that ensure academic support, mandatory reporting, and individualised support plans.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines (April 15): National, World, Sports Updates
Education.detail: News headlines for school assemblies covered national, world, and sports updates.

Lifestyle.headline: We're Seeing Polka Dots Everywhere This Spring—Here Are the 13 Pieces to Add to Your Wardrobe ASAP
Lifestyle.detail: Polka dots are trending in spring fashion, and Real Simple highlights 13 items to incorporate the pattern into your wardrobe.

Lifestyle.headline: The Century's Coolest Sports Watch Gets an Unexpected Sequel
Lifestyle.detail: Gear Patrol discusses a new iteration of a popular sports watch.

Lifestyle.headline: Vegemite is recognised globally – but how many people know Milo was invented in Australia?
Lifestyle.detail: An article discussing the origins of Milo, a popular malt beverage, and its connection to Australia.

Lifestyle.headline: Plastic detox: six kitchen upgrades to rid your food of microplastics and Pfas
Lifestyle.detail: The Guardian provides tips on upgrading your kitchen to reduce microplastics and PFAS in your food.

Lifestyle.headline: How a research scholar's curiosity about type designing led to a paper on Kerala's vibrant hand-painted political graffiti
Lifestyle.detail: An article in The Hindu explores a research scholar's work on the typography of hand-painted political graffiti in Kerala.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on three years of war in Sudan: a vast humanitarian crisis persists because the fighting does.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial highlights the ongoing humanitarian crisis in Sudan after three years of war, emphasizing the need for a resolution to the conflict.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on defence spending: should the UK's security rest with Donald Trump?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial questions whether the UK's security should rely on Donald Trump, specifically in regards to defense spending.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Supreme Court has no business meddling in Congress' impeachment power.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece argues against the Supreme Court's interference in Congress's impeachment powers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: President ensnared by social media and likely to be again
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial writer discusses how South Korean President Lee Jae Myung's social media activity makes him vulnerable to manipulation, citing a recent controversy involving a misleading video about Israeli forces.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: After Orban, Hungary faces an even harder battle
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discusses the challenges Hungary faces after Viktor Orban's defeat, including confronting the public demand for illiberal rule that sustained his power.

World.headline: US-Iran peace talks could resume in next two days, Trump says
World.detail: Former President Trump indicated that US-Iran peace talks might continue in Pakistan soon, amidst ongoing tensions and a US blockade of Iranian ports. The US and Iran are seeking a resolution, though mistrust remains.

World.headline: Israel and Lebanon hold rare talks in Washington, DC, amid Iran war
World.detail: Amidst the ongoing Iran war, Israeli and Lebanese envoys met in Washington, D.C., for rare direct negotiations. The U.S. is facilitating these discussions as part of broader efforts to de-escalate regional tensions.

World.headline: Hundreds missing after Rohingya boat capsizes in Andaman Sea: UN
World.detail: The United Nations reports that hundreds of Rohingya refugees are missing after a boat capsized in the Andaman Sea. Search and rescue efforts are underway, raising concerns about the safety and plight of refugees in the region.

World.headline: Premier League soccer player killed in attack on team bus in Ghana
World.detail: A Premier League soccer player was killed during an attack on a team bus in Ghana. Details are still emerging, but the incident has sent shockwaves through the sports community.

World.headline: Super Typhoon Sinlaku batters remote U.S. islands with ferocious winds
World.detail: Super Typhoon Sinlaku is impacting remote U.S. islands with strong winds and heavy rain. The storm is causing concerns for residents and infrastructure in the affected areas.

Zimbabwe.headline: Government moves to fix dilapidated housing stock
Zimbabwe.detail: The government is initiating measures to address the issue of deteriorating housing infrastructure across the country.

Zimbabwe.headline: Langford Estate land dispute stalls Fidelity housing project
Zimbabwe.detail: A land dispute at Langford Estate is causing delays in the commencement of a housing project by Fidelity, impacting potential homeowners.

Zimbabwe.headline: Nearly 1 000 women, young farmers to get financial, technical skills training
Zimbabwe.detail: Approximately 1,000 women and young farmers are set to receive financial and technical skills training to enhance their agricultural productivity and economic empowerment.

Zimbabwe.headline: Norton Town Council engages business community
Zimbabwe.detail: Norton Town Council is actively engaging with the local business community to foster collaboration and explore opportunities for economic development in the town.

Zimbabwe.headline: Security guard dies in robbery attack as Ward 17 calls for urgent action
Zimbabwe.detail: Following the death of a security guard during a robbery, residents of Ward 17 are demanding immediate action to improve security and prevent further incidents.
